channel.title: Comments for page "On the Slope of Olympus"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "On the Slope of Olympus"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As with "Parts", I am amazed at how fast the setting and situation are established; this tale surpasses "Parts" in the action sequence and the ungodly good way it conveys how two different gods perceive their world!

The author (in comments) seemed to wish the story were longer with more descriptions of certain aspects; it's like an exceptional 100m sprinter who wishes he could also excel at 800m - it's possible, but the gold medal for one is just as valuable as for the other!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Trying to describe action like this feels a bit like painting something abstract - a balancing act of using loose strokes so the viewer's imagination can fill in the gaps, while having just enough structure to stay coherent (I guess that's actually how I write everything, not just action scenes). I feel like I'm still working out that formula, but it's a lot of fun to experiment with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I'm so glad you liked it! I wanted to do a lot more than what made it in here - to give the characters a lot more depth, actually show the conflict between the MTF and Maria rather than just hint at it, get into how some Achilles Heel members/overseers feel about their situation in general, show a variety of greens coming out of the rift and how they're each dealt with, come up with something more twisted and memorable for the modernized version of their brain surgery… but I'm so slow that just getting what's here took the whole month.

So only the essentials made it in - worldbuilding for Achilles Heel, and a kind of "technical demo" for what reality bender combat could look like. And now we have all of 2018 and beyond to keep things going and eventually fill in those details :D I'm glad this worked out so well, and I'm already looking forward to the next holiday exchange.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That battle scene felt so perfectly surreal for what combat between reality benders would feel like. It got confusing at points but I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Utterly amazing, I’m so lucky that I got you as my giftor!

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
A tale about SCP-3480 and the glorious reality-bending violence that happens there. That skip and the tale Parts are background reading that put all this into context.

This was my gift exchange present for Modern_Erasmus - there must have been some magic in the spreadsheet of nondenominational holiday cheer (or Gaffney knows us really, really well) because it worked out perfectly as a chance to finally bring some ideas together that we'd been talking about for a while.
